 {"id":"beads_rust-b9o","title":"Routing + redirects (routes.jsonl + redirect file)","description":"# Routing + Redirects (routes.jsonl)

## Purpose
Implement classic cross-repo routing used by `show`, `update`, `close`, etc. This is **not** git automation; it only resolves which `.beads` directory to open for a given ID prefix.

## Key Artifacts
- `.beads/routes.jsonl` entries: `{ "prefix": "bd-", "path": "beads/mayor/rig" }`
- Town root: detected via `mayor/town.json` when walking up.
- `.beads/redirect`: overrides target beads dir for local setups.

## Resolution Rules
- Extract prefix from ID (substring before first `-`, plus hyphen).
- Search order:
  1) local `.beads/routes.jsonl`
  2) town root `.beads/routes.jsonl`
- If route found:
  - `path == "."` => town-level `.beads`
  - else resolve relative to town root
- If `.beads/redirect` exists in target, follow it (relative to current beads dir).
- If target missing/invalid, command errors.

## External Ref Derivation
- If prefix matches a route, it can map to `external:<project>:<id>` for external dependencies.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Prefix routing matches classic order and redirect behavior.
- Commands open routed DBs read-only or read/write as needed.

## Tests
- Route resolution with local + town root routes.jsonl.
- Redirect file override.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:18:16.726187846-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T02:23:11.841114304-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-b9o","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-1md","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:46:24.314227744-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-b9o","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ndl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:53:02.363538769-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-b9o","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rxg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T02:53:02.416140926-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-biw","title":"search Command Implementation (FTS5)","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br search` command for full-text search across issues using SQLite FTS5 (Full-Text Search 5). This enables fast, relevance-ranked searches across issue titles, descriptions, and comments.

## CLI Interface
```
br search <query> [OPTIONS]

Arguments:
  <query>                   Search query (supports FTS5 syntax)

Options:
  -t, --type <TYPE>         Filter by issue type (bug, feature, task, epic, chore)
  -s, --status <STATUS>     Filter by status (open, closed, in_progress, etc.)
  -p, --priority <PRIORITY> Filter by priority (0-4)
  -l, --label <LABEL>       Filter by label (can be repeated)
  --limit <N>               Maximum results (default: 50)
  --offset <N>              Skip first N results
  --json                    Output as JSON
  --robot                   Machine-readable output
```

## Technical Requirements

### FTS5 Virtual Table
```sql
-- Create FTS5 table for search
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues_fts USING fts5(
    id,
    title,
    description,
    -- Use porter stemmer for English
    tokenize = 'porter unicode61 remove_diacritics 2'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER issues_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON issues BEGIN
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(id, title, description)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.title, NEW.description);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_fts_delete AFTER DELETE ON issues BEGIN
    DELETE FROM issues_fts WHERE id = OLD.id;
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_fts_update AFTER UPDATE ON issues BEGIN
    DELETE FROM issues_fts WHERE id = OLD.id;
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(id, title, description)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.title, NEW.description);
END;
```

### Search Implementation
```rust
impl SqliteStorage {
    pub fn search_issues(&self, query: &str, filter: &SearchFilter) -> Result<Vec<IssueWithScore>> {
        // FTS5 query with BM25 ranking
        let sql = r#"
            SELECT 
                i.*,
                bm25(issues_fts) as score
            FROM issues i
            JOIN issues_fts f ON i.id = f.id
            WHERE issues_fts MATCH ?1
              AND (?2 IS NULL OR i.status = ?2)
              AND (?3 IS NULL OR i.issue_type = ?3)
              AND (?4 IS NULL OR i.priority = ?4)
            ORDER BY score
            LIMIT ?5 OFFSET ?6
        "#;
        
        // ... execute query
    }
    
    pub fn rebuild_fts_index(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
        // For initial population or repair
        self.conn.execute("DELETE FROM issues_fts", [])?;
        self.conn.execute(
            "INSERT INTO issues_fts(id, title, description) 
             SELECT id, title, description FROM issues",
            []
        )?;
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### FTS5 Query Syntax Support
Users can use FTS5 extended query syntax:
- Simple terms: `authentication bug`
- Phrase search: `"login failed"`
- Prefix search: `auth*`
- Boolean operators: `auth AND NOT password`
- Column filters: `title:authentication`
- NEAR operator: `NEAR(login password, 5)`

### Comments Search (Optional)
```sql
-- Separate FTS table for comments
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS comments_fts USING fts5(
    id,
    issue_id,
    content,
    tokenize = 'porter unicode61'
);
```

## Output Formats

### Human-readable
```
Search results for "authentication bug":

1. [bd-abc12] [BUG] P0 Authentication fails silently
   ...the authentication system throws no error when...
   Score: 0.95

2. [bd-def34] [FEATURE] P2 Add OAuth authentication
   ...implement OAuth authentication for external...
   Score: 0.72

Found 2 matches (50ms)
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "query": "authentication bug",
  "results": [
    {
      "id": "bd-abc12",
      "title": "Authentication fails silently",
      "issue_type": "bug",
      "priority": 0,
      "score": 0.95,
      "snippet": "...the authentication system throws no error when..."
    }
  ],
  "total": 2,
  "elapsed_ms": 50
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] FTS5 virtual table created during schema migration
- [ ] Triggers maintain FTS index on CRUD operations
- [ ] Search by title and description
- [ ] BM25 relevance ranking
- [ ] Filters combine with FTS (type, status, priority, labels)
- [ ] Pagination support (limit, offset)
- [ ] Snippet generation with term highlighting
- [ ] Human-readable and JSON output formats

 {"id":"beads_rust-c58","title":"Export Error Policies","description":"## Overview
Implement configurable error handling policies for JSONL export. Different use cases require different trade-offs between strictness and resilience.

## Error Policy Enum
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
pub enum ExportErrorPolicy {
    /// Abort export on any error (default for sync)
    #[default]
    Strict,
    
    /// Skip problematic records, export what we can
    BestEffort,
    
    /// Export valid records, report failures (for debugging)
    Partial,
    
    /// Only export issues (skip deps/labels/comments on error)
    RequiredCore,
}
```

## Technical Requirements

### Policy Implementation
```rust
pub struct ExportContext {
    pub policy: ExportErrorPolicy,
    pub errors: Vec<ExportError>,
    pub warnings: Vec<String>,
}

impl ExportContext {
    pub fn handle_error(&mut self, err: ExportError) -> Result<(), ExportError> {
        match self.policy {
            ExportErrorPolicy::Strict => {
                // Fail immediately
                Err(err)
            }
            ExportErrorPolicy::BestEffort => {
                // Log and continue
                tracing::warn!("Export error (skipping): {}", err);
                self.errors.push(err);
                Ok(())
            }
            ExportErrorPolicy::Partial => {
                // Record for report, continue
                self.errors.push(err);
                Ok(())
            }
            ExportErrorPolicy::RequiredCore => {
                // Fail only if its a core (issue) error
                match err.entity_type {
                    EntityType::Issue => Err(err),
                    _ => {
                        self.errors.push(err);
                        Ok(())
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
```

### Export With Policy
```rust
impl SqliteStorage {
    pub fn export_jsonl_with_policy(
        &self,
        output_dir: &Path,
        policy: ExportErrorPolicy,
    ) -> Result<ExportReport> {
        let mut ctx = ExportContext::new(policy);
        let mut report = ExportReport::new();
        
        // Export issues
        let issues = self.get_all_issues()?;
        let issues_path = output_dir.join("issues.jsonl");
        let mut issues_file = File::create(&issues_path)?;
        
        for issue in &issues {
            match serde_json::to_string(issue) {
                Ok(json) => {
                    writeln!(issues_file, "{}", json)?;
                    report.issues_exported += 1;
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    ctx.handle_error(ExportError {
                        entity_type: EntityType::Issue,
                        entity_id: issue.id.clone(),
                        message: e.to_string(),
                    })?;
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Export dependencies
        let deps = self.get_all_dependencies()?;
        // ... similar pattern
        
        // Export labels
        let labels = self.get_all_labels()?;
        // ... similar pattern
        
        // Export comments
        let comments = self.get_all_comments()?;
        // ... similar pattern
        
        report.errors = ctx.errors;
        report.policy_used = policy;
        Ok(report)
    }
}
```

### CLI Integration
```bash
# Default: strict (fail on any error)
br sync --flush-only

# Best effort: export what we can
br sync --flush-only --error-policy best-effort

# Partial: full report of failures
br sync --flush-only --error-policy partial

# Required core: issues must succeed, others can fail
br sync --flush-only --error-policy required-core
```

### Export Report
```rust
pub struct ExportReport {
    pub issues_exported: usize,
    pub dependencies_exported: usize,
    pub labels_exported: usize,
    pub comments_exported: usize,
    pub errors: Vec<ExportError>,
    pub policy_used: ExportErrorPolicy,
}

pub struct ExportError {
    pub entity_type: EntityType,
    pub entity_id: String,
    pub message: String,
}

impl ExportReport {
    pub fn has_errors(&self) -> bool {
        !self.errors.is_empty()
    }
    
    pub fn success_rate(&self) -> f64 {
        let total = self.issues_exported + self.dependencies_exported 
                  + self.labels_exported + self.comments_exported;
        let failed = self.errors.len();
        if total + failed == 0 { 1.0 }
        else { total as f64 / (total + failed) as f64 }
    }
}
```

### Output (Human)
```
Export completed with policy: best-effort

Exported:
  156 issues
  234 dependencies (2 errors)
  89 labels
  42 comments (1 error)

Errors (3):
  dependency bd-abc12 -> bd-xyz99: Invalid reference
  dependency bd-def34 -> bd-missing: Target not found
  comment c-123: Serialization failed
```

### Output (JSON)
```json
{
  "policy": "best-effort",
  "exported": {
    "issues": 156,
    "dependencies": 234,
    "labels": 89,
    "comments": 42
  },
  "errors": [
    { "type": "dependency", "id": "bd-abc12", "message": "Invalid reference" }
  ],
  "success_rate": 0.98
}
```

 {"id":"beads_rust-crf","title":"comment Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br comment` command for adding and viewing comments on issues. Comments provide a discussion thread for each issue.

## CLI Interface
```
br comment <COMMAND>

Commands:
  add <issue> <text>          Add comment to issue
  list <issue>                List comments on issue
  edit <comment-id> <text>    Edit existing comment
  delete <comment-id>         Delete comment (soft delete)

Options:
  --json                      Output as JSON
  --robot                     Machine-readable output
  --body-file <FILE>          Read comment text from file (use - for stdin)
```

## Implementation Details

### Database Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE comments (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    issue_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    body TEXT NOT NULL,
    author TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    deleted_at TEXT,  -- Soft delete
    FOREIGN KEY (issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_comments_issue ON comments(issue_id);
```

### Comment Operations
```rust
fn add_comment(&mut self, issue_id: &str, body: &str) -> Result<Comment> {
    let issue = self.resolve_id(issue_id)?;
    let now = Utc::now();
    
    let comment = Comment {
        id: self.next_comment_id()?,
        issue_id: issue.id.clone(),
        body: body.to_string(),
        author: self.current_user(),
        created_at: now,
        updated_at: now,
        deleted_at: None,
    };
    
    self.conn.execute(
        "INSERT INTO comments (issue_id, body, author, created_at, updated_at)
         VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
        params![comment.issue_id, comment.body, comment.author, 
                comment.created_at.to_rfc3339(), comment.updated_at.to_rfc3339()],
    )?;
    
    // Update issue's updated_at
    self.touch_issue(&issue.id)?;
    
    // Record event
    self.record_event(Event::CommentAdded { 
        issue_id: issue.id, 
        comment_id: comment.id 
    })?;
    
    Ok(comment)
}
```

### Markdown Support
Comments support GitHub-Flavored Markdown for rich formatting. The CLI doesn't render markdown, but it's preserved for rendering in web UIs or exported JSONL.

## Output Formats

### Comment List (Human-readable)
```
Comments on beads_rust-abc123 (3 total):

#1 by alice @ 2024-01-15 10:30
  Initial implementation looks good. One concern about
  the error handling in the auth flow.

#2 by bob @ 2024-01-15 14:22
  Good catch. I'll add explicit error types for auth failures.

#3 by alice @ 2024-01-16 09:15
  LGTM after the error handling changes.
```

```json
{
  "issue_id": "beads_rust-abc123",
  "comments": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "author": "alice",
      "body": "Initial implementation looks good...",
      "created_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

- [ ] Edit existing comments (preserve history via updated_at)

Comments enable asynchronous discussion about issues. This is essential for distributed teams and for agents that need to communicate findings or questions. The body-file option enables longer formatted comments.

-{"id":"beads_rust-d09","title":"label Command Group Implementation","description":"# label Command Group

## Purpose
Implement classic label management with correct JSON shapes, reserved label handling, and idempotent operations.

## CLI
```
br label add <issue...> <label>
br label remove <issue...> <label>
br label list [issue]
br label list-all
br label rename <old-name> <new-name>
```

## Flags
- `<issue...>`: One or more issue IDs (partial resolution supported).
- `<label>`: Label name (case-sensitive, alphanumeric + dash + underscore).
- `--json`: Output as JSON.
- `--robot`: Machine-readable output.

## Behavior

### label add
1. Resolve issue ID(s) via partial matching.
2. For each issue:
   - Check if label already exists (idempotent - no error if exists).
   - Validate label format (alphanumeric, dash, underscore).
   - Check for reserved prefix `provides:` - warn/reject.
   - Add label to issue.
   - Mark issue as dirty.
   - Emit `label_added` event.
3. Return results.

### label remove
1. Resolve issue ID(s).
2. For each issue:
   - Check if label exists (idempotent - no error if missing).
   - Remove label.
   - Mark issue as dirty.
   - Emit `label_removed` event.
3. Return results.

### label list [issue]
- If issue provided: return labels for that issue.
- If no issue: return all unique labels in the project.

### label list-all
Return all labels with issue counts:
```json
[
  {"label": "bug", "count": 15},
  {"label": "feature", "count": 8}
]
```

### label rename
1. Find all issues with old label.
2. For each: remove old, add new.
3. Atomic operation.

## Reserved Labels
- `provides:*` prefix is reserved for capability tracking.
- In br, reject with error or warn (configurable).

## Output

### JSON (add/remove)
```json
[
  {"status": "added", "issue_id": "bd-abc12", "label": "urgent"},
  {"status": "exists", "issue_id": "bd-def34", "label": "urgent"}
]
```

### JSON (list for issue)
```json
["bug", "priority-high", "backend"]
```

### JSON (list-all)
```json
[
  {"label": "bug", "count": 15},
  {"label": "feature", "count": 8},
  {"label": "urgent", "count": 3}
]
```

### Text Output (add)
```
✓ Added label urgent to bd-abc12
✓ Label urgent already exists on bd-def34
```

### Text Output (list-all)
```
Labels (3 total):
  bug (15 issues)
  feature (8 issues)
  urgent (3 issues)
```

## Error Handling
- **IssueNotFound**: If ID doesnt resolve → error with suggestions.
- **InvalidLabel**: If label contains invalid characters → error.
- **ReservedLabel**: If label starts with reserved prefix → error/warn.

## Logging
```rust
tracing::info!(issue_id = %id, label = %label, "Adding label");
tracing::debug!(already_exists = exists, "Label status check");
tracing::info!(issue_id = %id, label = %label, "Label added");
tracing::warn!(label = %label, "Attempted to add reserved label");
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON shapes match bd.
- Label casing preserved (case-sensitive).
- Add/remove are idempotent (no error if exists/missing).
- Reserved label handling matches bd behavior.

+{"id":"beads_rust-d09","title":"label Command Group Implementation","description":"# label Command Group

## Purpose
Implement classic label management with correct JSON shapes, reserved label handling, and idempotent operations.

## CLI
```
br label add <issue...> <label>
br label remove <issue...> <label>
br label list [issue]
br label list-all
br label rename <old-name> <new-name>
```

## Flags
- `<issue...>`: One or more issue IDs (partial resolution supported).
- `<label>`: Label name (case-sensitive, alphanumeric + dash + underscore).
- `--json`: Output as JSON.
- `--robot`: Machine-readable output.

## Behavior

### label add
1. Resolve issue ID(s) via partial matching.
2. For each issue:
   - Check if label already exists (idempotent - no error if exists).
   - Validate label format (alphanumeric, dash, underscore).
   - Check for reserved prefix `provides:` - warn/reject.
   - Add label to issue.
   - Mark issue as dirty.
   - Emit `label_added` event.
3. Return results.

### label remove
1. Resolve issue ID(s).
2. For each issue:
   - Check if label exists (idempotent - no error if missing).
   - Remove label.
   - Mark issue as dirty.
   - Emit `label_removed` event.
3. Return results.

### label list [issue]
- If issue provided: return labels for that issue.
- If no issue: return all unique labels in the project.

### label list-all
Return all labels with issue counts:
```json
[
  {"label": "bug", "count": 15},
  {"label": "feature", "count": 8}
]
```

### label rename
1. Find all issues with old label.
2. For each: remove old, add new.
3. Atomic operation.

## Reserved Labels
- `provides:*` prefix is reserved for capability tracking.
- In br, reject with error or warn (configurable).

## Output

### JSON (add/remove)
```json
[
  {"status": "added", "issue_id": "bd-abc12", "label": "urgent"},
  {"status": "exists", "issue_id": "bd-def34", "label": "urgent"}
]
```

### JSON (list for issue)
```json
["bug", "priority-high", "backend"]
```

### JSON (list-all)
```json
[
  {"label": "bug", "count": 15},
  {"label": "feature", "count": 8},
  {"label": "urgent", "count": 3}
]
```

### Text Output (add)
```
✓ Added label urgent to bd-abc12
✓ Label urgent already exists on bd-def34
```

### Text Output (list-all)
```
Labels (3 total):
  bug (15 issues)
  feature (8 issues)
  urgent (3 issues)
```

## Error Handling
- **IssueNotFound**: If ID doesnt resolve → error with suggestions.
- **InvalidLabel**: If label contains invalid characters → error.
- **ReservedLabel**: If label starts with reserved prefix → error/warn.

## Logging
```rust
tracing::info!(issue_id = %id, label = %label, "Adding label");
tracing::debug!(already_exists = exists, "Label status check");
tracing::info!(issue_id = %id, label = %label, "Label added");
tracing::warn!(label = %label, "Attempted to add reserved label");
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- JSON shapes match bd.
- Label casing preserved (case-sensitive).
- Add/remove are idempotent (no error if exists/missing).
- Reserved label handling matches bd behavior.

Implement field-level validation rules for all entities (issues, dependencies, labels, comments). Validation ensures data integrity and provides clear error messages when constraints are violated.

## Validation Rules from Documentation

### Issue Fields
```rust
pub struct IssueValidator;

impl IssueValidator {
    pub fn validate(issue: &Issue) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {
        let mut errors = Vec::new();
        
        // ID: Required, matches prefix pattern, max 50 chars
        if issue.id.is_empty() {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("id", "cannot be empty"));
        }
        if issue.id.len() > 50 {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("id", "exceeds 50 characters"));
        }
        if !is_valid_id_format(&issue.id) {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("id", "invalid format (expected prefix-hash)"));
        }
        
        // Title: Required, max 500 chars
        if issue.title.is_empty() {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("title", "cannot be empty"));
        }
        if issue.title.len() > 500 {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("title", "exceeds 500 characters"));
        }
        
        // Description: Optional, max 100KB
        if let Some(ref desc) = issue.description {
            if desc.len() > 102400 {
                errors.push(ValidationError::field("description", "exceeds 100KB"));
            }
        }
        
        // Status: Must be valid enum value
        // (Handled by Status::from_str)
        
        // Priority: 0-4 range
        if issue.priority > 4 {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("priority", "must be 0-4"));
        }
        
        // IssueType: Must be valid enum value
        // (Handled by IssueType::from_str)
        
        // Timestamps: created_at <= updated_at
        if issue.updated_at < issue.created_at {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("updated_at", "cannot be before created_at"));
        }
        
        // ExternalRef: Optional, max 200 chars, no spaces
        if let Some(ref ext) = issue.external_ref {
            if ext.len() > 200 {
                errors.push(ValidationError::field("external_ref", "exceeds 200 characters"));
            }
            if ext.contains(char::is_whitespace) {
                errors.push(ValidationError::field("external_ref", "cannot contain whitespace"));
            }
        }
        
        if errors.is_empty() {
            Ok(())
        } else {
            Err(errors)
        }
    }
}

fn is_valid_id_format(id: &str) -> bool {
    // Format: prefix-hash (e.g., "bd-abc123")
    let parts: Vec<&str> = id.splitn(2, "-").collect();
    if parts.len() != 2 {
        return false;
    }
    let prefix = parts[0];
    let hash = parts[1];
    
    // Prefix: 1-10 lowercase alphanumeric
    if prefix.is_empty() || prefix.len() > 10 || !prefix.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_lowercase() || c.is_ascii_digit()) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // Hash: 3-8 lowercase alphanumeric
    if hash.len() < 3 || hash.len() > 8 || !hash.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_lowercase() || c.is_ascii_digit()) {
        return false;
    }
    
    true
}

### Dependency Rules
```rust
pub struct DependencyValidator;

impl DependencyValidator {
    pub fn validate(dep: &Dependency, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<(), Vec<ValidationError>> {
        let mut errors = Vec::new();
        
        // Self-dependency not allowed
        if dep.issue_id == dep.depends_on_id {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("depends_on_id", "issue cannot depend on itself"));
        }
        
        // Both issues must exist
        if !storage.id_exists(&dep.issue_id)? {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("issue_id", "issue not found"));
        }
        if !storage.id_exists(&dep.depends_on_id)? {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("depends_on_id", "dependency target not found"));
        }
        
        // Cycle detection
        if storage.would_create_cycle(&dep.issue_id, &dep.depends_on_id)? {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("depends_on_id", "would create dependency cycle"));
        }
        
        // Duplicate check
        if storage.dependency_exists(&dep.issue_id, &dep.depends_on_id)? {
            errors.push(ValidationError::field("depends_on_id", "dependency already exists"));
        }
        
        // Dependency type: Must be valid
        // (Handled by DependencyType::from_str)
        
        if errors.is_empty() {
            Ok(())
        } else {
            Err(errors)
        }
    }
}

### Label Rules

pub struct LabelValidator;

impl LabelValidator {
    pub fn validate(label: &str) -> Result<(), ValidationError> {
        // Non-empty
        if label.is_empty() {
            return Err(ValidationError::field("label", "cannot be empty"));
        }
        
        // Max 50 chars
        if label.len() > 50 {
            return Err(ValidationError::field("label", "exceeds 50 characters"));
        }
        
        // Allowed characters: alphanumeric, hyphen, underscore
        if !label.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == "-" || c == "_") {
            return Err(ValidationError::field("label", "invalid characters (only alphanumeric, hyphen, underscore allowed)"));
        }
        
        Ok(())
    }
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, thiserror::Error)]
pub struct ValidationError {
    pub field: String,
    pub message: String,
}

impl ValidationError {
    pub fn field(field: &str, message: &str) -> Self {
        Self {
            field: field.into(),
            message: message.into(),
        }
    }
}

impl std::fmt::Display for ValidationError {

+## Sync Safety (br): Threat Model and Guardrails
+
+This section defines the safety envelope for `br sync`. It exists because a real-world incident class
+showed that a sync tool can accidentally delete all source files by touching the wrong paths or
+performing git operations implicitly. `br` must be **non-invasive by design and enforcement**.
+
+### Incident Class This Plan Prevents
+
+**Observed failure pattern (from another project):**
+- A sync command produced a commit that deleted the entire source tree.
+- Recovery required restoring files from a known-good commit.
+
+**Key lesson:** any sync feature that can touch paths outside `.beads/` or run git commands can
+catastrophically damage the working tree. `br` must *prove* it cannot do that.
+
+### Threat Model
+
+**Threat actors (not malicious, but dangerous):**
+- **User error:** wrong CLI flags, wrong repo root, wrong JSONL path, confusion over env vars.
+- **Configuration drift:** `BEADS_JSONL` or metadata points outside `.beads/`.
+- **Filesystem quirks:** symlinks, path traversal (`..`), absolute paths, or Windows drive roots.
+- **Corrupted inputs:** conflict markers or malformed JSONL.
+- **Runtime failures:** disk full, permission denied, crash mid-write.
+- **Hidden integrations:** implicit git operations, hooks, or daemons.
+
+**Failure modes to eliminate:**
+1. **Path escape:** export/import writes outside `.beads/`.
+2. **Implicit git activity:** any git commit, stage, hook, or config change.
+3. **Partial writes:** export truncates JSONL or import corrupts DB on failure.
+4. **Silent overrides:** data loss without explicit user intent.
+5. **Undetected corruption:** conflict markers or invalid JSONL accepted.
+
+### Non-Goals (Explicit)
+
+`br sync` must **never**:
+- run git commands or modify `.git/`
+- install or trigger git hooks
+- auto-commit or auto-push
+- run a daemon or background process
+
+### Safety Invariants (Testable)
+
+These are the must-hold invariants for every sync operation:
+
+1. **No git operations:** sync code must not execute git or touch `.git/`.
+2. **Strict path allowlist:** sync IO may only target `.beads/` paths and an explicitly
+   opt-in external JSONL path (never by default).
+3. **Atomic export:** export writes to a temp file in the same directory, fsyncs, then atomically
+   renames; existing JSONL is preserved on failure.
+4. **Import safety:** import aborts on conflict markers or invalid JSONL; schema/prefix validation
+   is enforced by default.
+5. **Explicit user intent:** any override or risky operation requires explicit `--force` or
+   opt-in flags.
+6. **Observable decisions:** safety-critical decisions are logged at debug level with
+   sanitized, non-sensitive detail.
+
+### Guardrails and Mitigations (Mapping)
+
+| Failure Mode | Primary Guardrail |
+| --- | --- |
+| Path escape / wrong JSONL path | Canonical path validation + allowlist + opt-in for external paths |
+| Implicit git activity | No git dependencies in sync; hard guard against git invocation |
+| Partial JSONL write | Temp-file write + fsync + atomic rename |
+| Corrupt/merged JSONL | Conflict marker detection + per-line JSON validation |
+| Data loss via override | Explicit `--force` gating + clear warnings |
+| Hidden failure cause | Structured debug logs for each check and decision |
+
+### Safety UX Principles
+
+- **Fail fast, fail safe:** if any safety check fails, do nothing and explain why.
+- **User intent is explicit:** defaults are safe; risks require flags.
+- **Transparency:** provide clear error messages and verbose logging guidance.
+
+### Test Requirements (Enforced by Plan)
+
+Every invariant must be enforced by:
+- **Unit tests** (path guards, conflict detection, atomic write logic)
+- **Integration tests** (no repo file changes, preflight rejection)
+- **E2E scripts** with full log capture and artifacts for postmortems
+
+---
+
+## Sync Safety Spec (Behavior + Test Mapping)
+
+This spec translates the invariants into concrete behavior, flags, and test coverage.
+It is intentionally explicit so implementation and tests do not require external context.
+
+### CLI Semantics (Safe Defaults)
+
+1. **Mode selection is explicit**: `br sync` must require exactly one of:
+   - `--flush-only` (export)
+   - `--import-only` (import)
+2. **External JSONL paths are opt-in only**:
+   - If `BEADS_JSONL` or metadata points outside `.beads/`, require an explicit opt-in flag
+     (e.g., `--allow-external-jsonl`) or fail.
+3. **Risky operations require `--force`**:
+   - Exporting when DB is empty but JSONL is non-empty
+   - Exporting when JSONL appears newer or contains more issues than DB
+   - Importing with prefix mismatch or conflicting metadata
+4. **No implicit git behavior**: `br sync` must never run git commands or touch `.git/`.
+
+### Preflight (Read-Only, Fail-Fast)
+
+Before any write, sync must run a preflight that:
+- Confirms `.beads/` exists and is the active workspace
+- Validates JSONL path via canonicalized allowlist
+- Rejects conflict markers in JSONL for import
+- Validates JSONL readability (per-line JSON)
+- Logs each check at debug level with sanitized paths
+
+If any check fails: **abort with no side effects**.
+
+### Export (Flush) Behavior
+
+- Write to a temp file in the same directory as JSONL
+- fsync temp file, then atomically rename into place
+- Preserve existing JSONL on failure
+- Ensure temp cleanup only touches allowlisted paths
+- Log phases: preflight → write → fsync → rename → finalize
+
+### Import Behavior
+
+- Refuse conflict markers
+- Validate JSONL per line (schema-level)
+- Enforce prefix/metadata compatibility unless `--force`
+- Execute DB writes in a transaction; rollback on error
+- Log phases: preflight → scan → validate → upsert → finalize
+
+### Safety Logging Requirements
+
+All safety-critical decisions must emit debug logs:
+- Path allowlist decisions (allowed/rejected)
+- Preflight check results
+- Guardrail triggers (e.g., stale DB, empty DB)
+- Import/export phases and rollbacks
+
+Logs must avoid sensitive content and include sanitized paths.
+
+### Invariant → Guardrail → Test Mapping
+
+| Invariant | Guardrail | Unit Tests | Integration Tests | E2E Scripts |
+| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
+| No git operations | No git deps, hard guard, .git untouched | N/A | Ensure .git unchanged | Verify no commits/staged changes |
+| Strict path allowlist | Canonical path checks, opt-in external | Path traversal cases | Unsafe path preflight rejection | Full repo snapshot diff |
+| Atomic export | Temp + fsync + rename | Export atomicity tests | Failure injection (readonly dir) | Export artifact validation |
+| Import safety | Conflict scan + JSON validation | Conflict marker tests | Reject conflict markers | Import with conflict marker file |
+| Explicit user intent | `--force` gating | Flag parsing tests | Guardrail messaging | Dangerous scenarios require opt-in |
+| Observable decisions | Structured debug logs | Log capture tests | Logs on failure | Logs + artifacts archived |
+
